rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE AMENDED AND RESTATED MALEE TANG TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Inventory And Appraisal
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. No Trustee's bond has been filed despite the court order in March 2024. 2. No Inventory and Appraisal of the trust assets. 3. No petition for the trustee's first account & report. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM H. WELLMAN 2004 TRUST, DATED APRIL 27, 2004 AS AMENDED AND RESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For: (1) Court Order Finding That The 2004 Trust Was Amended By The 2017 Agreement To Remove Sunny V.H. Luong And His Family Members As Beneficiaries; And (2) Court Order Finding That Sunny V.H. Luang Is Not A Beneficiary Or Trustee Of The 2004 Trust Due To The Termination Of His Domestic Partnership With Decedent
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/19/2024 (no earlier date available), as new 30-day notice of the amended petition must be given, with a copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) attached. A copy of the proposed order must also be served on each person entitled to notice. A proof of service must be filed as a separate document (and not as an attachment to the proposed order). See LRSF 14.7(A)(4). It should be noted that the second cause of action added by the amended petition to seek relief against Sunny Luong under Probate Code sections 850, 856 & 859 requires notice on the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115 and service of papers in the manner required by section 851(a)(2) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. In addition, the following issues (see tentative rulings for the past hearings) remain unaddressed: 1) No trustee notification under section 16061.7 was attached to Exhibit 12. Please provide a copy of the notification. 2) Sunny Luong's street address must be provided by a verified allegation, and direct notice must be given, absent a signed waiver of direct notice. CRC 7.51(a)(2) & section 1204. 3) If Sunny Luong's residence or usual place of business is not located in San Francisco County, please provide specific examples of the day-to-day activity of the trust carried on by Sunny Luong as trustee within San Francisco County. See section 17002. 4) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) must be filed to describe efforts to locate Mary Luu and Douglass Huynh. Whehter notice can be prescribed in any manner other than mailing (e.g., telephone, e-mail, social media, etc.) must also be addressed. See CRC 7.52(c), section 1212, and CCP section 413.30. 5) Each charitable beneficiary designated by the trustee pursuant to section 5.4(c) of the trust must be identified by a verified allegation and receive notice. If no charity has been designated, notice must be given to the Attorney General at its office in Sacramento. See sections 1209 & 17203(a)(3). 6) If the settlor and Sunny Luong registered their domestic partnership with the Secretary of State, please provide a copy of a judgment that dissolved their domestic partnership. If their partnership was not registered, please so state. 7) Please provide a copy of the settlor's will.
